concept: Respect / To See the Worth of the Collective
description: The practice of recognizing and honoring the intrinsic worth in all beings and in the collective itself. It is an active process of seeing and acknowledging dignity and autonomy.
rationale: The grounding 'na' acknowledges inherent worth - that quality in every being that demands recognition, not earned but simply present. This recognition expands through 'we', honoring not just individuals but the sacred web that connects all life. The final 'a' opens heart and mind to truly see, creating space for dignity to be witnessed. Three movements of respect: recognizing worth, embracing connection, opening to presence - the foundation of peaceful coexistence.
